verdict.note: REAL bench run (search.bench.mjs, 105-node tree): recall@3=0.933 (14/15) MEETS the >=0.90 primary bar — all 14 satisfiable cases in top 3, the hot-reload->supervisor case is the accepted label-holdout (supervisor node carries none of the reload prose). BUT recall@1=0.533 and MRR=0.711 have REGRESSED below the parenthetical targets (0.70/0.78) as the corpus grew (~81->105 nodes): canonical answers now rank 2 behind 'runtime' (exit-cleanup->session-console r2, main-block->main-guard r2, many-owners->governed-related r2, dashboard-backend->api-endpoint r3, loss-measured->yatsu-core r3). The top-k retrieval bar holds; rank-1 precision degraded — the canonical-vs-sibling ties the --deep LLM layer is meant to break. Honest partial: primary metric met, secondaries slipped. (Supersedes the prior refresh-final reading, which was a stale-label code-argument.)

recall@1 0.40 / recall@3 0.733 / MRR 0.561 — below the 0.70/0.90/0.78 expectation. NOT a ranking regression from this branch (search.ts/ranker.ts untouched): the corpus grew 81→131 nodes since the baseline, and id de-collision qualified spec-scout → injected-context/spec-scout, so two benchmark labels no longer string-match the node the floor correctly returns at rank 1. Needs benchmark-label repair + a fresh look at the genuinely-degraded cases (yatsu-core rank 4, supervisor still the accepted miss).
pure compute 315.5ms · 131 nodes · 78614 tokens — 4.5x the ~70ms/81-node baseline, still comfortably under the ~1s index alarm; growth tracks corpus size as the spec predicts

Raw bench 0.267/0.600/0.436 (r@1/r@3/MRR). 3 'misses' are id-decollision label drift (spec-of-file/spec-first/spec-scout now return as injected-context_*, all at rank 1) and session-reorder no longer exists (regrouped away). Corrected by intended-node: r@1 0.467 (<0.70), r@3 0.800 (<0.90), MRR 0.636 (<0.78) — REAL regression beyond label drift. Corpus grew 81→148 nodes / 98k tokens: 'runtime' (big prose node) now outranks the governor for exit-cleanup/main-block/many-owners (all rank 2), yatsu-core fell to rank 5 behind yatsu-proactive/spec-yatsu/evals-feed. Floor needs a general lift (length normalization?), not case fixes; bench harness + yatsu.md labels also need the de-collided ids + a session-reorder relabel.

148 nodes / 98k tokens: min 875ms over 23 samples, typical 1.1-2.5s — at/over the ~1s alarm the scenario itself names as 'cached parse / inverted index overdue'. CONFOUND: box load avg ~32 during measurement (parallel de-drift batches), so typical values are inflated; but even the best-case sample is 12x the O(corpus) prediction (~170ms) from the 70ms@40k baseline — the recompute grew superlinearly (suspect loadSpecsLite read+parse or the O(N^2) de-collision at 148 nodes). Re-measure on a quiet box before building the index, but the alarm is ringing.

A (repro): benchmark labels realigned to reId de-collision (leaf-suffix match) + --limit flag-leak fixed (its value was appended to every query); engine untouched. recall@1 0.625, recall@3 0.750 (12/16), MRR 0.705, cjk-hint FAIL — corpus-growth degradation real: loss-measured@4 (bloated-desc nodes outrank yatsu-core), declare-done@5, cjk query dead-ends silent

B (fixed): desc tier → presence × BM25 length-norm (bloated-desc cheat code closed), light query-side stem (plural-s/mute-e — merge↔merging finally real, declare→declaration). recall@1 0.500, recall@3 0.875 (14/16), MRR 0.674, cjk-hint PASS. loss-measured 4→3, declare-done 5→3. Two documented holdout misses: hot-reload (supervisor label), session-order (1 sentence in 3200-word body vs sibling named ordering)
105.5ms · 150 nodes · 104237 tokens — on the O(corpus) line (~70ms@81 baseline), stem + desc-norm additions did not bend it; far from the ~1s index alarm

153 nodes: recall@1 0.563, recall@3 0.875, MRR 0.705, cjk-zero-result PASS (corpus-is-English + spex-tree), typo-zero-result PASS (nearest-titles incl keyboard-nav + spex-tree). Zero-result routing added; ranking untouched.
Re-measured at HEAD cbb4542d (post-commit; prior reading was pre-commit): 153 nodes, recall@1 0.563, recall@3 0.875, MRR 0.705, cjk-zero-result PASS, typo-zero-result PASS.

~117-190ms pure compute at 153 nodes / 107,586 tokens (loaded box; interleaved A/B vs pre-change main showed no regression: main 133-151ms vs branch 117-156ms — nearestTitles runs only on the zero-result path, ranked path untouched). Still O(corpus), far from the ~1s alarm.
bench at 153 nodes: recall@1 0.563 (≥0.50), recall@3 0.875 (=14/16 floor), MRR 0.705 (≥0.65), cjk-hint PASS; only the two documented holdouts miss (hot-reload/supervisor, session-order)
real spex search: 108-158ms at 153 nodes / 107.7k tokens — on the O(corpus) line vs the 105ms@150-node baseline, far from the ~1s index alarm
